title: Lace executor manifest (lace-executor.toml)
description: TOML structure declaring how the testkit invokes a specific executor. Placed in the executor's repo root. The testkit reads it via `lace-conformance -m <path>` (or auto-discovers `./lace-executor.toml`) before running the conformance suite.

version.description: Executor version. Reported in test output.

language.description: Implementation language (e.g. python, javascript, kotlin, c). Free-form.

conforms_to.description: Lace spec version range this executor claims to satisfy (semver range e.g. '>=0.9.1'). Testkit may refuse to run if its bundled vectors are for a version this executor doesn't claim.

Adapter.description: Command templates for the three CLI subcommands. Each template is a shell command with placeholders `{script}`, `{vars}`, `{vars_list}`, `{context}`, `{prev}`, `{config}`. The testkit substitutes paths for placeholders and invokes the command, captures stdout, parses as JSON, and diffs against expected.

parse.description: Command to parse a script. Must accept a script file path (placeholder `{script}`) and write JSON to stdout: `{ "ast": <AST conforming to ast.schema.json> }` on success or `{ "errors": [<ExpectedError>...] }` on parse failure. Exit 0 on success, 1 on parse error.

validate.description: Command to validate a script. Accepts `{script}`, optionally `{vars_list}` (JSON array of declared variable names) and `{context}` (JSON ValidationContext). Stdout JSON: `{ "errors": [...], "warnings": [...] }`. Exit 0 if errors empty, else 1.

run.description: Command to execute a script. Accepts `{script}`, `{vars}` (JSON variable map), optionally `{prev}` (path to prev result JSON), `{config}` (path to lace.config). Stdout: ProbeResult JSON conforming to result.schema.json. Exit 0 on outcome=success, 1 on failure, 2 on timeout, 3 on internal error.

timeout_seconds.description: Per-invocation timeout. Testkit kills the subprocess after this many seconds and reports the test as failed (timeout).

env.description: Environment variables to set when invoking the executor. Useful for build-output paths or feature flags.

Conformance.description: Declares the spec features this executor *does not* implement. The testkit skips vectors that require an omitted feature and reports a `compliant-partial` label instead of `compliant`. Default: empty list (full conformance). See lace-spec.md §16.
